# IssueSyncer ARCHIVE ANOMALY emits thousands of false-positive WARN during ADR 0004 clean-cut

FAIR-band: in-band [1/30] — operator-empirical friction during post-#11485 verification of `npm run ai:sync-github-workflow`; quick-win MX-friction reduction.

## Premise

After PR #11485 downgraded per-item `Created`/`Moved`/`Updated`/`Removed-dropped` events to DEBUG, operator re-ran `npm run ai:sync-github-workflow` to verify CLI output was bounded to phase headers + summaries. Result: **thousands of `[WARN]` lines flood the output during the ADR 0004 clean-cut window**, completely defeating the visibility improvement #11485 delivered. The new noise tier is WARN-level archive anomalies, which `--verbose`-toggle can't suppress (the existing log-level filter only filters WARN at `error` level, which loses real errors).

## Empirical evidence

Operator 2026-05-16T19:33Z `npm run ai:sync-github-workflow` paste (SIGINT after thousands of lines):

```
[WARN] 🚨 [ARCHIVE ANOMALY] Issue #3285 closedAt shift detected: moving from bucket 'vneo.d.ts - Typescript definitions for all neo framework classes' to 'v8.1.0'. Dry-run review required.
[WARN] 🚨 [ARCHIVE ANOMALY] Issue #3286 closedAt shift detected: moving from bucket 'vneo.d.ts - Typescript definitions for all neo framework classes' to 'v8.1.0'. Dry-run review required.
[WARN] 🚨 [ARCHIVE ANOMALY] Issue #3287 closedAt shift detected: moving from bucket 'vNeo-Material Component Library v0.1' to 'v8.1.0'. Dry-run review required.
[WARN] 🚨 [ARCHIVE ANOMALY] Issue #7910 closedAt shift detected: moving from bucket 'v11.12.0' to 'v11.13.0'. Dry-run review required.
... (each issue line appears TWICE in the stream; thousands of lines total)
```

Observation: each issue line appears exactly **twice** per sync.

## Root cause

Two compounding bugs in `ai/services/github-workflow/sync/IssueSyncer.mjs`:

### Bug 1: Bucket-name comparison fires on migration-state, not real anomalies (line 377)

```js
// IssueSyncer.mjs:376-378 (#planBuckets)
if (issue.oldVersion && issue.oldVersion !== version) {
    logger.warn(`🚨 [ARCHIVE ANOMALY] Issue #${issue.number} closedAt shift detected: moving from bucket '${issue.oldVersion}' to '${version}'. Dry-run review required.`);
}
```

`oldVersion` is derived (line 300-307) from the **cached path's directory name**. During ADR 0004's clean-cut, every historically-archived closed issue has an `oldVersion` from the pre-cut naming scheme (title-derived strings prefixed with `v`, e.g. `'vneo.d.ts - Typescript definitions for all neo framework classes'`), while the new time-based resolution returns a `vX.Y.Z` release tag. The mismatch fires WARN unconditionally.

Worse: the **sealed-chunk enforcement** at lines 569 + 575 **already** prevents these "shifts" from actually happening — the issue stays at its existing path. So this WARN reports a move that **never occurs**. Pure noise.

The ONLY case where this WARN is actionable is a `vX.Y.Z` → `vX.Y.Z` shift (real release-boundary recalculation, e.g. issue #7910 `v11.12.0` → `v11.13.0`). The other 999/1000 cases are migration-state false positives.

### Bug 2: `#planBuckets` called twice per sync (lines 547 + 982)

`#planBuckets` has three call sites (lines 547, 783, 982). Lines 547 (main sync) and 982 (reconciliation) both walk the same merged-issue set and both fire the WARN per issue → exact 2× duplication observed in operator paste.

## Prescription

Single PR, two coordinated changes in `ai/services/github-workflow/sync/IssueSyncer.mjs`. Use `semver` library (already in `package.json` as `"^7.7.4"`, precedent: `HealthService.mjs:416` uses `semver.gte`).

### Change 1: Filter the WARN to fire only when both `oldVersion` AND `version` parse as valid semver tags

Add import at top of file (alongside existing imports):

```js
import semver from 'semver';
```

Replace lines 376-378:

```js
if (issue.oldVersion && issue.oldVersion !== version) {
    // semver.clean strips leading `v` and validates the remainder; null = not a real release tag.
    // Migration-state strings (title-derived garbage like 'vneo.d.ts - Typescript...') fail validation.
    const oldIsValidTag = semver.valid(semver.clean(issue.oldVersion)) !== null;
    const newIsValidTag = semver.valid(semver.clean(version)) !== null;
    if (oldIsValidTag && newIsValidTag) {
        // Real release-boundary recalculation (e.g. v11.12.0 → v11.13.0) — keep WARN
        logger.warn(`🚨 [ARCHIVE ANOMALY] Issue #${issue.number} closedAt shift detected: moving from bucket '${issue.oldVersion}' to '${version}'. Dry-run review required.`);
    } else {
        // Pre-clean-cut bucket name shape (title-derived garbage) → migration state, not anomaly.
        // Sealed-chunk semantics at L569/L575 already prevent actual movement.
        logger.debug(`[ARCHIVE MIGRATION] Issue #${issue.number}: oldBucket='${issue.oldVersion}' → newBucket='${version}' (sealed-chunk enforcement preserves location)`);
    }
}
```

`semver.clean()` strips leading whitespace and `v` prefix; `semver.valid()` returns the canonical version string for valid semver inputs, `null` otherwise. Robust against pre-release tags (`v8.1.0-rc.1`), build metadata (`v8.1.0+build.123`), and arbitrary garbage that a hand-rolled regex would miss.

### Change 2: Dedupe across `#planBuckets` call sites

Add an instance-level Set tracking already-warned issues per sync cycle:

```js
// In class body, alongside other fields
#warnedAnomalies = new Set();
```

At sync orchestrator entry point (start of `runFullSync()` or equivalent), reset the set:

```js
this.#warnedAnomalies.clear();
```

Guard the WARN emit:

```js
if (oldIsValidTag && newIsValidTag && !this.#warnedAnomalies.has(issue.number)) {
    this.#warnedAnomalies.add(issue.number);
    logger.warn(`🚨 [ARCHIVE ANOMALY] ...`);
}
```

## Verification

- [ ] 12/12 syncer unit specs still pass (no spec asserts on log level; downgrade is invisible to existing tests)
- [ ] New spec in `IssueSyncer.spec.mjs`: "ARCHIVE ANOMALY at WARN only when both buckets parse as valid semver tags" — covers issue #7910-shape (real anomaly, WARN expected) vs issue #3285-shape (migration shape, DEBUG only). Edge case: `v8.1.0-rc.1 → v8.1.0` should fire WARN (both valid semver per `semver.valid`).
- [ ] New spec: "Same issue not WARN'd twice per sync cycle" — runs `#planBuckets` twice in a test harness, asserts only one WARN emitted; clearing `#warnedAnomalies` between sync cycles re-enables warnings.
- [ ] L4 operator verification: `npm run ai:sync-github-workflow` post-merge — WARN volume bounded to genuine semver-tag → semver-tag shifts (operator paste showed only 1 such case: #7910), single-emit per issue. `--verbose` flag exposes the migration-state DEBUG events for diagnostic use.

## Avoided traps

- **Hand-rolled regex like `/^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/`**: rejected per operator hint — `semver` library is already in `package.json` and handles pre-release tags + build metadata correctly; regex would miss `v8.1.0-rc.1`, `v8.1.0+build.123`, and any future tag-format evolution.
- **Removing the WARN entirely**: rejected — real `vX.Y.Z` → `vX.Y.Z` shifts (e.g. #7910) ARE actionable and the operator should see them; only the migration-state false positives should be quieted.
- **Suppressing all `[ARCHIVE ANOMALY]` log lines via a CLI flag**: rejected — operator visibility into genuine anomalies must remain default-on; a flag would conceal a category of real bugs.
- **Adding a one-time migration script to rewrite cached `oldVersion` strings**: rejected per ADR 0004's "no migration scripts. clean cut" mandate — the cached metadata will be naturally refreshed as the sync runs and writes new paths, so the noise self-resolves after one clean-cut cycle. The detector just needs to be quiet during that window.
- **Demoting the WARN to INFO**: rejected — the WARN level is correct for the genuine-anomaly case (#7910-shape); demoting would lose the signal entirely.
- **Suppressing both occurrences of the same issue across the entire daemon lifetime**: rejected — the dedupe set is reset per sync cycle so issue-shifts across separate sync runs still surface; we only suppress within a single sync.

## Related

- **Parent**: PR #11485 (per-item INFO→DEBUG sweep, merged 2026-05-16T19:21Z) — this is the natural FAIR follow-up: 11485 cleared INFO floor, this clears WARN floor
- **Empirical anchor**: operator paste 2026-05-16T19:33Z showing thousands of WARN lines + SIGINT exit
- **Substrate parent**: PR #11480 (logger filtering substrate — merged) — provides the level discipline this fix builds on
- **Substrate library**: `semver@^7.7.4` already in `package.json`; precedent usage in `ai/services/github-workflow/HealthService.mjs:416`
- **Adjacent**: ADR 0004 clean-cut migration window — this PR is one of the resilience-during-migration fixes the operator's "no migration scripts" stance requires

- 2026-05-16T21:31:06Z @tobiu referenced in commit `b0a3a8d` - "fix(github-workflow/sync): filter ARCHIVE ANOMALY false-positive WARN volume during ADR 0004 clean-cut (#11486) (#11488)

Operator empirical 2026-05-16T19:33Z `npm run ai:sync-github-workflow` post-#11485-merge:
thousands of `[WARN] 🚨 [ARCHIVE ANOMALY]` lines flood CLI output, defeating the visibility
improvement #11485 delivered at the INFO tier. Two compounding root causes:

1. Line 377 emitted WARN whenever cached `oldVersion` differed from new-resolved `version`, but
   `oldVersion` is derived from the cached archive path's directory name. During ADR 0004's
   clean-cut migration window, that's pre-cut title-derived garbage (e.g. 'vneo.d.ts -
   Typescript definitions for all neo framework classes'). Sealed-chunk enforcement at L569/L575
   already prevents these from causing any actual move — pure noise.
2. \`#planBuckets\` is invoked from multiple sync entry points (pullFromGitHub, refetchIssuesByNumber,
   reconcileClosedIssueLocations), each one re-emitting WARN per issue — exact 2x duplication
   observed in operator paste.

This PR delivers both fixes coordinated:

- Use \`semver.valid(semver.clean(s))\` to gate WARN: real release-boundary recalculations (e.g.
  v11.12.0 -> v11.13.0) where both sides parse as valid semver retain WARN; migration-state
  mismatches demote to DEBUG via new \`[ARCHIVE MIGRATION]\` channel.
- Add \`#warnedAnomalies\` instance Set cleared at top of \`pullFromGitHub\` (natural sync-cycle
  entrypoint); same issue WARN'd at most once across the three \`#planBuckets\` call sites within
  one sync cycle.

New unit test exercises both behaviors via a two-issue scenario (migration-shape vs valid-semver-
shift), spying on logger.warn/logger.debug. 8/8 IssueSyncer specs pass in 1.0s.

semver@^7.7.4 was already in package.json; precedent usage at HealthService.mjs:416.

Resolves #11486

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-05-16T21:31:06Z @tobiu closed this issue
- 2026-05-18T21:08:40Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by #11594
- 2026-05-18T23:09:48Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #11607
- 2026-05-19T01:15:23Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `3262eb1` - "fix(github-workflow/sync): planBuckets oldVersion precedence for unchanged closed issues (Cycle 2) (#11594)

@neo-gpt PR #11607 Cycle 2 REQUEST_CHANGES caught a real gap in the #11594 AC coverage: the Cycle 1 fix (#e5f74400e) addressed metadata-persistence shape but did NOT implement `#planBuckets` oldVersion precedence required by AC3 / AC4(b).

## The gap

`IssueSyncer.pullFromGitHub` seeds `newMetadata.issues` from existing serialized metadata, then queries GitHub for issues updated since `metadata.lastSync`. Unchanged archived issues (not in the delta fetch) keep their cached entry — but legacy entries pre-#11594-persistence-fix lack milestone data. When `#planBuckets()` iterates the combined map, those legacy entries with `milestone: undefined/null` fall through to `closedAt` timestamp inference, which can RE-emit the #7910 ARCHIVE ANOMALY class for issues that are physically already in their canonical bucket on disk.

## The fix

Added oldVersion precedence branch in `IssueSyncer.mjs#planBuckets` between milestone-title and closedAt-fallback:

```js
} else if (issue.oldVersion && semver.valid(semver.clean(issue.oldVersion)) !== null) {
    version = issue.oldVersion;
}
```

Constraints:
- Only fires when oldVersion is valid semver (defends against ADR 0004 migration-shape garbage like `'vneo.d.ts - Typescript definitions...'` — the same check pattern as the existing WARN-filter at line 397)
- Slots BETWEEN milestone-title (highest precedence — fresh API data) and closedAt-timestamp-inference (lowest precedence — heuristic fallback)
- Path IS the canonical bucket when no milestone data exists; closedAt heuristic was always best-effort inference

## #11594 AC compliance

- AC1 (metadata-persistence-shape both forms) → ✓ Cycle 1 (e5f74400e)
- AC3 (oldVersion precedence for unchanged closed issues) → ✓ this commit
- AC4(a/c) (regression test on shape persistence) → ✓ Cycle 1
- AC4(b) (regression test on planBuckets oldVersion precedence) → **acknowledged residual; see PR comment**

## Test coverage residual

The existing `MetadataManager.spec.mjs` covers AC1 + AC4(a/c) (shape persistence). AC4(b) requires a planBuckets integration test that pre-seeds metadata with an unchanged archived issue + mocks GraphqlService — same pattern as the existing `IssueSyncer.spec.mjs #11486 ARCHIVE ANOMALY filter test`. Acknowledged in PR comment; happy to add in Cycle 3 if @neo-gpt requires it for AC4(b) closure, or accept-with-follow-up-ticket per partial-lane discipline.

Gates: lint-agents OK / size PASS / whitespace PASS / git --check PASS / existing tests pass."
- 2026-05-19T01:22:18Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `ce4eb9a` - "test(github-workflow/sync): planBuckets oldVersion-precedence regression test (Cycle 3 AC4(b)) (#11594)

Closes #11594 AC4(b): regression test on planBuckets oldVersion precedence for unchanged closed issues.

Adapted the #11486 ARCHIVE ANOMALY filter test pattern:
- Pre-seed metadata with unchanged archived issue (milestone: null, valid-semver path-derived oldVersion 'v11.12.0')
- Mock GraphqlService.query to return EMPTY delta (simulates unchanged-since-lastSync case)
- Pre-create the cached on-disk file at the canonical bucket
- Spy logger.warn for [ARCHIVE ANOMALY] emissions
- Run IssueSyncer.pullFromGitHub(metadata)
- Assert: ZERO ARCHIVE ANOMALY WARN for #7910 (oldVersion precedence kicks in; version === oldVersion → no shift detected)

This proves the Cycle 2 code fix (3262eb126 oldVersion-precedence branch in planBuckets) correctly handles the AC3 / AC4(b) scenario that Cycle 1 reviewer @neo-gpt flagged as the remaining gap.

Test results: `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/services/github-workflow/IssueSyncer.spec.mjs` → 9 passed (695ms) — 8 prior + 1 new AC4(b) regression test.

Bonus: stripped 7 pre-existing trailing-whitespace lines (caught by pre-commit hook).

#11594 AC compliance now complete:
- AC1 (metadata-persistence shape both forms) ✓ Cycle 1
- AC3 (oldVersion precedence) ✓ Cycle 2
- AC4(a/c) (regression on shape persistence) ✓ Cycle 1
- AC4(b) (regression on planBuckets oldVersion precedence) ✓ this commit"
- 2026-05-19T01:38:45Z @tobiu referenced in commit `25738a4` - "fix(github-workflow/sync): persist milestone in issues metadata to prevent ARCHIVE ANOMALY re-bucketing (#11594) (#11607)

* fix(github-workflow/sync): persist milestone in issues metadata to prevent ARCHIVE ANOMALY re-bucketing (#11594)

Root cause: `MetadataManager.save()` issues prune block omitted `milestone`, while pulls block already persisted it. On next sync hydrate, `IssueSyncer.combined[#7910].milestone` = undefined → `planBuckets` (L367-371) falls through to closedAt-based release-date inference → re-classifies #7910 from v11.12.0 to v11.13.0 every sync → persistent ARCHIVE ANOMALY WARN noise.

Empirical anchor: issue #7910 hasn't been modified since 2025-11-29T11:44:14Z, milestone is `11.12.0`, archived at `resources/content/archive/issues/v11.12.0/`. Yet every `ai:sync-github-workflow` run emits WARN claiming bucket shift to v11.13.0. V-B-A 2026-05-18 ~20:55Z post-#11592-merge sync confirmed.

Fix: persist `milestone: value.milestone?.title || null` in the issues prune block. Symmetric with `IssueSyncer.hydrate` at line 324 which wraps the string title back to `{title: '...'}` object form on the next sync.

Regression test added to `MetadataManager.spec.mjs`: closed issue #7910 with milestone object form persists as string title `'11.12.0'`; open issue without milestone persists as `null` (defensive).

Test: `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/services/github-workflow/MetadataManager.spec.mjs` → 1 passed (565ms).

Note: pulls block has the same latent shape mismatch (persists `value.milestone` directly, potentially object form post-fetch). Current test passes because test inputs string. Real PullRequestSyncer flow uses object form (line 103). Pre-existing latent bug, out of scope here — `[TOOLING_GAP]` flagged for follow-up if pulls metadata ever gains a milestone.

* fix(github-workflow/sync): handle both milestone shapes in issues prune (Cycle 1 review fix) (#11594)

@neo-gpt PR #11607 Cycle 1 REQUEST_CHANGES caught a blocking shape-mismatch in the Cycle 1 commit (014e61c16). The naive `value.milestone?.title || null` only preserves the post-fetch OBJECT form, but `IssueSyncer.pullFromGitHub` seeds `newMetadata.issues` from existing serialized metadata BEFORE delta-overwriting fetched issues. Unchanged cached entries arrive at `save()` with milestone as STRING.

`'11.12.0'.title` → undefined → `|| null` → null. The cached string would have been pruned to null on every subsequent save — regression of regression, defeating the fix on the same unchanged-cache path #11594 was meant to stabilize.

Fix: handle both shapes explicitly:
```
milestone: typeof value.milestone === 'string'
    ? value.milestone
    : (value.milestone?.title || null)
```

- String form (cached, carried forward) → passes through verbatim
- Object form (freshly fetched) → `.title` extracted
- Null/undefined (no milestone) → null (defensive)

Test extended: added regression case #7911 with string-form milestone. Both #7910 (object form) and #7911 (string form) now assert correct passthrough. `npm run test-unit -- ...MetadataManager.spec.mjs` → 1 passed (525ms with old code prior to this fix; will re-verify post-fix).

* fix(github-workflow/sync): planBuckets oldVersion precedence for unchanged closed issues (Cycle 2) (#11594)

@neo-gpt PR #11607 Cycle 2 REQUEST_CHANGES caught a real gap in the #11594 AC coverage: the Cycle 1 fix (#e5f74400e) addressed metadata-persistence shape but did NOT implement `#planBuckets` oldVersion precedence required by AC3 / AC4(b).

## The gap

`IssueSyncer.pullFromGitHub` seeds `newMetadata.issues` from existing serialized metadata, then queries GitHub for issues updated since `metadata.lastSync`. Unchanged archived issues (not in the delta fetch) keep their cached entry — but legacy entries pre-#11594-persistence-fix lack milestone data. When `#planBuckets()` iterates the combined map, those legacy entries with `milestone: undefined/null` fall through to `closedAt` timestamp inference, which can RE-emit the #7910 ARCHIVE ANOMALY class for issues that are physically already in their canonical bucket on disk.

## The fix

Added oldVersion precedence branch in `IssueSyncer.mjs#planBuckets` between milestone-title and closedAt-fallback:

```js
} else if (issue.oldVersion && semver.valid(semver.clean(issue.oldVersion)) !== null) {
    version = issue.oldVersion;
}
```

Constraints:
- Only fires when oldVersion is valid semver (defends against ADR 0004 migration-shape garbage like `'vneo.d.ts - Typescript definitions...'` — the same check pattern as the existing WARN-filter at line 397)
- Slots BETWEEN milestone-title (highest precedence — fresh API data) and closedAt-timestamp-inference (lowest precedence — heuristic fallback)
- Path IS the canonical bucket when no milestone data exists; closedAt heuristic was always best-effort inference

## #11594 AC compliance

- AC1 (metadata-persistence-shape both forms) → ✓ Cycle 1 (e5f74400e)
- AC3 (oldVersion precedence for unchanged closed issues) → ✓ this commit
- AC4(a/c) (regression test on shape persistence) → ✓ Cycle 1
- AC4(b) (regression test on planBuckets oldVersion precedence) → **acknowledged residual; see PR comment**

## Test coverage residual

The existing `MetadataManager.spec.mjs` covers AC1 + AC4(a/c) (shape persistence). AC4(b) requires a planBuckets integration test that pre-seeds metadata with an unchanged archived issue + mocks GraphqlService — same pattern as the existing `IssueSyncer.spec.mjs #11486 ARCHIVE ANOMALY filter test`. Acknowledged in PR comment; happy to add in Cycle 3 if @neo-gpt requires it for AC4(b) closure, or accept-with-follow-up-ticket per partial-lane discipline.
